title: skopeo copy, trying to reuse blob. How to purge this to start over fresh?
body:
Ok so Im having an issue installing Ansible Automation Platform. But my question revolves around a skopeo copy error.
Here is the command syntax:

skopeo copy --retry-times 5 --src-creds 'username:password' --src-tls-verify=True --dest-creds username:password --dest-tls-verify=false docker://registry.redhat.io/ansible-automation-platform-23/ee-supported-rhel8:latest docker://use2-aaphubap01.domain.net/ee-supported-rhel8:latest
Getting image source signatures
Checking if image destination supports signatures
FATA[0003] copying system image from manifest list: trying to reuse blob sha256:762908e901a628d2f6f7967c6f0ea9c3723c0c53318592508c518772afaa417d at destination: Get "automationhub.domain.net/token?account=admin&scope=repository%3Aee-supported-rhel8%3Apull%2Cpush&service=use2-aaphubap01.domain.net": unsupported protocol scheme ""

First problem, the url 'automationhub.domain.net' is a dest value from a previous run. I believe it's being carried over from the "previous blob" reference, is true how do I purge that history?
Second problem, the _unsupported protocol scheme_, what does this mean?

skopeo version 1.9.4-dev

NAME="Red Hat Enterprise Linux"
VERSION="9.1 (Plow)"
